sections.paragraph:
λύτρωσις, εως, ἡ (s. λυτρόω; as legal and commercial t.t. in pap)

① experience of being liberated from an oppressive situation, transf. sense of commercial usage ‘redemption of someth. for a price’: ransoming, releasing, redemption (Plut., Arat. 11, 2 λ. αἰχμαλώτων; Palaeph. exc. Vat. p. 99, 10; LXX; TestJos 8:1) ποιεῖν λύτρωσιν τῷ λαῷ bring about a deliverance for the people Lk 1:68 (cp. TestLevi 2:10 Armenian vers.: s. Charles). προσδέχεσθαι λύτρωσιν Ἰερουσαλήμ wait for the redemption of Jerusalem 2:38; αἰωνία λ. Hb 9:12. διὰ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ κυρίου λ. ἔσται πᾶσιν τοῖς πιστεύουσιν redemption will come 1 Cl 12:7.

② abstr. for concr. ransom(-money) δώσεις λ. ἁμαρτιῶν σου you must give a ransom for your sins D 4:6; cp. B 19:10.—M-M. TW. Spicq.

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

transf. transf. = transferred

someth. someth. = something

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arat Arat of Soli in Cilicia, IV–III b.c.—List 5

Palaeph Palaeph , date uncertain—List 5

exc. exc. = except

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

vers. vers. = version(s)

Charles Charles = RC., Revelation (ICC); also used in connection with Test12Patr—Lists 2, 6

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

abstr. abstr. = abstractum pro concreto (abstract for the concrete)

concr. concr. = concrete(ly), cp. abstr.

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 606
